reforms.description: The Irish government has set a target to increase economy-wide research and development (R&D) spending to 2.5% of gross national product (GNP) by 2013, aiming to enhance the innovation environment and attract foreign research activities. This includes plans to improve the science framework to encourage foreign firms to shift some of their research activities to Ireland, rebalancing public support for business R&D towards market-driven measures.
reforms.component_name: R&D Spending Target and Framework Improvement
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending and improving the framework for innovation are expected to enhance productivity and attract foreign investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The target was set for 2013, indicating it is a planned reform.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in R&D funding and support mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: To ensure that R&D is more commercially focussed, public support for business R&D could be rebalanced away from direct grants and towards market-driven measures.

reforms.description: The Programme for Research in Third Level Institutions (PRTLI) has significantly invested in upgrading Ireland's science infrastructure, allocating funding on a competitive basis to universities and technical institutes, which has led to the establishment of major research centres and increased research capacity. Additionally, Science Foundation Ireland (SFI) provides substantial funding for research in biotechnology and ICT, focusing on peer-reviewed grants that support researchers and collaborative efforts between academia and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in research infrastructure and funding enhances research capacity and collaboration, which supports innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The PRTLI and SFI funding have been operational since 2000.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent major investments in research infrastructure and funding mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: This changed in the late 1990s when the government substantially boosted funding for research and development (R&D) and set up several granting agencies to deliver it.

reforms.description: Enterprise Ireland has shifted its focus to provide more targeted product-specific R&D support for small enterprises, enhancing the links between universities and industry. The Strategic Innovation Fund created by the 2006 Budget aims to increase investment in higher education, allocating €300 million over five years to enhance the capacity and quality of research and innovation in the sector.
reforms.package_name: Support for Small Enterprises and Higher Education
reforms.component_name: Enterprise Ireland R&D Support and Strategic Innovation Fund
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Targeted R&D support for small enterprises and increased investment in higher education are expected to foster innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Strategic Innovation Fund was established in 2006 and has been operational since then.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant but narrower changes in support mechanisms for small enterprises and higher education.
reforms.source_quote: With an additional €251 per student per year, the Strategic Innovation Fund created by the 2006 Budget will not fundamentally change the situation.
